title: Today's Tech Brief: A Simpsons Writer's Presidential Bid and Tech's Latest
summary: A Simpsons writer runs for president, and other tech news.
imageAlt: Image from source article for ‘It’s Not a Joke’: A ‘Simpsons’ Writer Holds His First Presidential Campaign Rally
imageCaption: ‘It’s Not a Joke’: A ‘Simpsons’ Writer Holds His First Presidential Campaign Rally
imageSourceTitle: ‘It’s Not a Joke’: A ‘Simpsons’ Writer Holds His First Presidential Campaign Rally

body.text: I'm looking at a story that's got a bit of everything: politics, entertainment, and a dash of tech. Dan Greaney, a writer for The Simpsons, recently held his first presidential campaign rally in Los Angeles. About 30 people showed up, including some interesting costumes - I'm talking Uncle Sam, Darth Vader, and Elsa from Frozen. This isn't a joke; Greaney is serious about his bid. My read is that this is as much about entertainment value as it is about politics.
More News: Quantum Computing Advances and a New AI Model

In other news, there's been some significant progress in quantum computing. A team of researchers just announced a major breakthrough in developing a more stable quantum bit, or qubit. This could be a game-changer for the field, enabling more practical applications of quantum computing. Separately, a new AI model is being touted as a major improvement over existing language models. It's supposed to be better at understanding nuance and context, which could lead to some pretty cool - and maybe a little unsettling - advancements in AI capabilities.

As I wrap up this brief, I'm struck by the diversity of stories in the tech world. From a Simpsons writer's presidential bid to advances in quantum computing and AI, there's always something new and interesting to explore. For me, it's a reminder that tech is all around us, influencing every aspect of our lives - even politics and entertainment.

sources.title: ‘It’s Not a Joke’: A ‘Simpsons’ Writer Holds His First Presidential Campaign Rally
